recursiveIndexedAccessSimplification.tlua(5,5): error TS1110: Type expected.
recursiveIndexedAccessSimplification.tlua(5,9): error TS1005: ';' expected.
recursiveIndexedAccessSimplification.tlua(5,9): error TS2693: 'Recur' only refers to a type, but is being used as a value here.
recursiveIndexedAccessSimplification.tlua(5,15): error TS2304: Cannot find name 'T'.
recursiveIndexedAccessSimplification.tlua(5,18): error TS2693: 'number' only refers to a type, but is being used as a value here.
recursiveIndexedAccessSimplification.tlua(5,26): error TS1011: An element access expression should take an argument.
recursiveIndexedAccessSimplification.tlua(5,27): error TS1005: ';' expected.
recursiveIndexedAccessSimplification.tlua(8,12): error TS1109: Expression expected.
recursiveIndexedAccessSimplification.tlua(8,13): error TS2695: Left side of comma operator is unused and has no side effects.
recursiveIndexedAccessSimplification.tlua(8,23): error TS100035: A '...' expression is only allowed inside a vararg function.
recursiveIndexedAccessSimplification.tlua(8,26): error TS1005: ']' expected.
recursiveIndexedAccessSimplification.tlua(8,27): error TS1128: Declaration or statement expected.


==== recursiveIndexedAccessSimplification.tlua (12 errors) ====
    // https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/63270
    
    type Recur<T> =
        (T extends  (unknown[]) ? {} : { [K in keyof T]?: Recur<T[K]>}) |
        [...Recur<T>[number][]];
        ~
!!! error TS1110: Type expected.
            ~~~~~
!!! error TS1005: ';' expected.
            ~~~~~
!!! error TS2693: 'Recur' only refers to a type, but is being used as a value here.
                  ~
!!! error TS2304: Cannot find name 'T'.
                     ~~~~~~
!!! error TS2693: 'number' only refers to a type, but is being used as a value here.
                             
!!! error TS1011: An element access expression should take an argument.
                              ~
!!! error TS1005: ';' expected.
    
    function join<T>(l: Recur<T>[]): Recur<T> {
        return ['marker', ...l];
               ~
!!! error TS1109: Expression expected.
                ~~~~~~~~
!!! error TS2695: Left side of comma operator is unused and has no side effects.
                          ~~~
!!! error TS100035: A '...' expression is only allowed inside a vararg function.
                             ~
!!! error TS1005: ']' expected.
                              ~
!!! error TS1128: Declaration or statement expected.
    }
    
    function a<T>(l: Recur<T>[]): void {
        local x: Recur<T> | undefined = join(l);
    }
